ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'chocolate cake' class (FOODON_03301710); no specific 'double chocolate blackout cake' entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers cakes and pastries broadly; no specific chocolate blackout cake code exists. Closest parent category anchor used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code for chocolate cake or blackout cake found in clinical terminology; not applicable for this food type.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical slice of Double Chocolate Blackout Cake weighs approximately 120 g, representing a standard dessert portion as served in a café or restaurant setting.

nutrient_source.title: Double Chocolate Blackout Cake — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (G00010542)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database record G00010542 for Double Chocolate Blackout Cake. The HPB record describes this item as served with dark chocolate sauce and not with ice cream. Energy value of 42 kcal/100g is notably low for a cake and likely reflects the sauce-dominant composition of the as-served portion rather than the full cake; this is preserved as reported. Saturated fat not separately reported by HPB; set to -1. Monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, and trans fat not reported by HPB; set to -1.

gi_source.title: Chocolate cake, commercially prepared — GI value 38–65 range; chocolate fudge cake GI 38 (Atkinson et al., 2008); chocolate cake with frosting GI 38–65 per University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Double Chocolate Blackout Cake in the University of Sydney GI Database. The closest analogous entry is chocolate cake with frosting (GI ~38–65, medium category). Given the high sugar content (9.94g/100g) and near-zero fibre (0g/100g), the upper bound of 65 is applied as a conservative estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × available carbohydrate per serving (10.36g/100g × 120g serving = 12.43g) ÷ 100 = 8.1. Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94g/100g) with negligible fibre and potassium; insulin-mediated sodium retention risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this high-sugar, low-fibre dessert, as excess added sugar intake promotes insulin-driven sodium retention and raises cardiovascular risk independent of direct sodium content (AHA 2018 guidelines).

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Near-pure sugar carbohydrate profile (9.94g sugar out of 10.36g total carbohydrate per 100g) with zero dietary fibre, driving rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this item, as the carbohydrate is almost entirely free sugar with no fibre to moderate absorption, producing rapid postprandial glucose spikes; if consumed, restrict to a very small portion and pair with a protein source.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar content with negligible fibre, potassium, and micronutrients; likely saturated fat from butter and chocolate in the full cake preparation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should treat this as an occasional indulgence only, given the high free-sugar load, likely saturated fat content from butter and chocolate, and absence of cardioprotective nutrients such as fibre, potassium, or omega-3 fatty acids.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Double Chocolate Blackout Cake is a rich Western-origin layered chocolate dessert cake served in Singapore café and restaurant settings. The dish is described as accompanied by a dark chocolate sauce and is explicitly not served with ice cream. The 'blackout' style refers to a deeply chocolate-forward preparation — typically a moist chocolate sponge layered with chocolate pudding or ganache filling and coated in chocolate crumbs or frosting — originating from Ebinger's Bakery in Brooklyn, New York. The HPB Singapore Foodo database records this item with an unusually low energy density of 42 kcal per 100g, which likely reflects the as-served composition being sauce-dominant or a diluted portion rather than the full dense cake. The carbohydrate profile is almost entirely sugar (9.94g sugar out of 10.36g total carbohydrate per 100g) with zero dietary fibre, making this a high-glycaemic-impact dessert despite the low absolute caloric density. Protein is reported as 0g per 100g by HPB, consistent with a sauce-dominant serving where the chocolate sauce contributes minimal protein. Fat is 0.25g per 100g, also consistent with a diluted sauce-based serving. Sodium is low at 3mg per 100g. From a clinical perspective, the near-pure sugar carbohydrate profile with zero fibre makes this unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The high free-sugar content promotes rapid postprandial glucose elevation and insulin-mediated sodium retention. The food is classified as processed, contains gluten, dairy, and eggs as allergens, and is vegetarian but not vegan or halal-certified.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (G00010542), an approved primary regional source with confidence 0.95. The record is marked as reviewed and canonical. GI is estimated from the closest analogous University of Sydney GI Database entry (chocolate cake with frosting) due to absence of a direct measurement, which reduces GI confidence to medium; overall record confidence remains high given the exact HPB source match for all nutrient fields.
notes: ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION (g4): protein_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB Singapore Foodo (G00010542). This is confirmed zero per the HPB source and is consistent with a sauce-dominant serving where the dark chocolate sauce contributes negligible protein at the reported dilution. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 by HPB, confirmed zero per source. dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB, confirmed zero per source — the dark chocolate sauce preparation contains no fibre-contributing ingredients at detectable levels. magnesium_mg is reported as 0.0 by HPB, confirmed zero per source. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (g3): saturated_fat_g set to -1: HPB Foodo record G00010542 does not separately report saturated fat for this item. monounsaturated_fat_g set to -1: not reported by HPB for this item. polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: not reported by HPB for this item. trans_fat_g set to -1: not reported by HPB for this item. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g all set to -1: micronutrient and water data not reported by HPB Foodo for this record. ENERGY ANOMALY NOTE: The reported energy of 42 kcal/100g is atypically low for a chocolate cake (typical range 350–450 kcal/100g). This is preserved as reported from HPB and likely reflects the as-served portion being sauce-dominant or a diluted sauce serving rather than the full dense cake. Users should verify serving size accuracy with the HPB data provider. HALAL STATUS: Not confirmed as halal-certified; flag set to false pending verification. GI NOTE: GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database chocolate cake entries; no direct measurement for Double Chocolate Blackout Cake exists in the approved GI database.
